task.name: Self-upgrade this tmux-cli session and continue the conversation
task.objective: Rebuild tmux-cli from source, install it, and restart THIS Claude session onto the fresh binary WITHOUT losing the conversation â€” so the relaunched Claude spawns a NEW MCP server that reflects the upgraded binary. The invoking session cannot restart itself in place (Ctrl-C does NOT exit Claude Code, and MCP tools are frozen at Claude startup), so the upgrade is performed by a DETACHED helper that: builds â†’ prewrites the bypass-dialog config â†’ kills this session â†’ recreates it with `tmux-cli start-attach --resume <conversation-id>`, which relaunches Claude resuming the same conversation on the new binary. Every step below was proven live; do not improvise around them.

input.note: $ARGUMENTS (optional): `--source <dir>` to pin the tmux-cli source tree to rebuild (else resolved from `TMUX_CLI_SRC`, `self_update.source_dir` in setting.yaml, or the current dir if it is the tmux-cli repo). `--build-cmd "<cmd>"` to override the default build (`make install`). No args â†’ rebuild from the resolved source with `make install` and restart this session.

requirements.req: MUST be invoked from INSIDE a tmux-cli session (a resolvable `#{session_name}` and a Claude conversation).

A NATIVE TERMINAL MUST exist (`$DISPLAY` set + konsole/gnome-terminal/xterm/alacritty/kitty/wezterm): `start-attach` ATTACHES, so the recreate needs a real TTY to attach to. With no GUI, STOP and hand the operator the recovery command instead of killing the session (a headless kill+start-attach dead-ends â€” start-attach's `tmux attach` fails "not a terminal").

The tmux-cli SOURCE tree MUST be resolvable and BUILD CLEAN â€” the build runs BEFORE the session is killed, and a build failure ABORTS without killing (never leave a dead session behind a broken build).

glossary.term: The invoking session's project dir (`pwd`, or `$TMUX_CLI_PROJECT_PATH`). `start-attach` recreates THIS dir's session; it is also the project entry whose bypass-version config is prewritten.
The tmux-cli source tree to rebuild (`--source` / `TMUX_CLI_SRC` / `self_update.source_dir` / REPO if REPO is the tmux-cli repo). Often == REPO when self-upgrading from the tmux-cli checkout.
This tmux session's exact name (`tmux display-message -p '#{session_name}'`). Killed by EXACT name only â€” never `pkill -f`.

The CLAUDE CONVERSATION ID to resume â€” the newest `~/.claude/projects/<escaped-REPO>/<id>.jsonl` (escaped-REPO = REPO with `/`â†’`-`). This is the id Claude prints as "Resume this session with: claude --resume <id>". It is NOT the tmux window UUID (`@window-uuid`), which can DIVERGE from the conversation id â€” resuming by the window UUID errors "No conversation found" and strands the session (the historical dead-end).
The running Claude Code version (`claude --version`, e.g. `2.1.201`) â€” prewritten into the bypass-version config so the recreated Claude skips the "Bypass Permissions mode â€” accept?" dialog.

llm.mandate: NEVER RESTART IN PLACE. Ctrl-C does NOT exit Claude Code (it interrupts the turn), so `send-keys C-c` + a relaunch command lands the command in Claude's input box, not a shell â€” the process is never replaced. The ONLY working restart is to KILL THE WHOLE TMUX SESSION and recreate it with `start-attach`. Proven: an in-place kill of just the Claude process also works but does not refresh session-level state; the session recreate is the sanctioned path.

RESUME BY CONVERSATION ID, NOT WINDOW UUID. Pass CONV_ID (newest jsonl) to `tmux-cli start-attach --resume <CONV_ID>`. The window UUID (`@window-uuid`) is NOT guaranteed to equal the conversation id; using it errors "No conversation found with session ID" and, because the session was already killed, dead-ends the conversation. Resolve CONV_ID from the jsonl, never from `@window-uuid`.

RUN DETACHED (setsid) â€” THE HELPER MUST SURVIVE KILLING ITS OWN SESSION. The kill of MYSESS destroys the invoking pane; a foreground helper dies with it and never runs `start-attach`. Launch the whole buildâ†’killâ†’recreate sequence as a `setsid` process (its own session/process group, reparented to init), with stdio detached, so it outlives this Claude. `start-attach` itself runs inside a freshly-spawned native terminal (konsole -e â€¦) that provides the TTY it attaches.

PREWRITE THE BYPASS-DIALOG CONFIG. Claude Code re-shows the "Bypass Permissions mode â€” accept?" dialog after an auto-update whenever its running version > the seen version â€” gated by `~/.claude.json` top-level `lastReleaseNotesSeen` and per-project `projects[REPO].lastVersionBase` (NOT `bypassPermissionsModeAccepted`, which stays true). start-attach's normal path does not seed these, so BEFORE the recreate, atomically write `lastReleaseNotesSeen=VER`, `projects[REPO].lastVersionBase=VER`, `projects[REPO].hasTrustDialogAccepted=true`, `projects[REPO].hasCompletedProjectOnboarding=true`, `bypassPermissionsModeAccepted=true`. Do the prewrite AFTER killing MYSESS (this session's Claude, the in-project writer, is then dead) to minimize the concurrent-writer race. Without it the recreated Claude stalls on the dialog and dead-ends.

BUILD BEFORE KILL; ABORT ON BUILD FAIL. Run the build+install FIRST while this session is still alive. If it fails (non-zero), LOG and ABORT â€” do NOT kill the session. Only a green build proceeds to kill+recreate.
SAFETY: NEVER KILL WITHOUT A RESUME TARGET. Verify `<CONV_ID>.jsonl` exists immediately before killing; if missing, ABORT (killing would strand the conversation with nothing to resume).

ANNOUNCE + RECOVERY COMMAND. Before launching the helper, tell the operator the screen will close and a new terminal will reappear continuing the conversation, and print the manual recovery command: `cd <REPO> && tmux-cli start-attach --resume <CONV_ID>`. Then STOP â€” this Claude is about to be replaced.
NEVER `pkill -f` â€” kill MYSESS by EXACT name (`tmux kill-session -t <MYSESS>`); a cmdline-pattern kill can SIGTERM the helper itself.

step.title: Resolve handles (read-only)
step.action: REPO = `pwd` (or `$TMUX_CLI_PROJECT_PATH`). MYSESS = `tmux display-message -p '#{session_name}'`. VER = `claude --version` leading semver. CONV_ID = basename of the NEWEST `~/.claude/projects/<REPO with / â†’ ->/*.jsonl` (strip `.jsonl`). SRC = `--source` arg, else `TMUX_CLI_SRC`, else `self_update.source_dir` from setting.yaml, else REPO.
Print the resolved handles so the operator can eyeball them before anything destructive.

step.title: Preconditions â€” abort loudly on any miss
step.action: Assert: in a tmux session (MYSESS non-empty); CONV_ID resolved AND its jsonl exists; a native terminal exists (`$DISPLAY` + a terminal binary on PATH); SRC resolvable. If a native terminal is missing, STOP: print the recovery command and instruct the operator to run it in their terminal (headless self-upgrade would dead-end).

step.title: Compose the detached self-upgrade helper (a bash script)
step.action: Write a helper script that runs, in order, logging to a file under the scratchpad: 1. `sleep 6` (let this invoking turn finish rendering). 2. Re-verify `<CONV_ID>.jsonl` exists â€” else ABORT. 3. BUILD: `cd <SRC> && <build-cmd, default: make install>`. On non-zero exit, LOG "build failed â€” aborting, session preserved" and EXIT (do not kill). 4. KILL: `tmux kill-session -t <MYSESS>` (exact name). 5. PREWRITE ~/.claude.json atomically (temp-file + rename): set the five bypass/trust keys per the mandate, with VER. 6. RECREATE in a native terminal: `DISPLAY=$DISPLAY setsid <terminal> -e bash -c "cd '<REPO>' && tmux-cli start-attach --resume '<CONV_ID>'; exec bash"` â€” start-attach recreates MYSESS's dir, reuses CONV_ID as the supervisor window UUID, and Claude relaunches `--resume <CONV_ID>` (no dialog, conversation resumed) on the new binary.

step.title: Launch detached + announce, then STOP
step.action: Launch the helper fully detached: `DISPLAY=$DISPLAY setsid bash <helper> </dev/null >/dev/null 2>&1 &` then `disown`.

Announce to the operator: the build runs (~30-60s), then this session is killed and reappears in a new terminal continuing the conversation on the fresh binary; print the recovery command `cd <REPO> && tmux-cli start-attach --resume <CONV_ID>`. Then STOP â€” do not continue working; this Claude is about to be replaced.

execution-rules.rule: Build BEFORE kill; abort (session preserved) on build failure.
Kill MYSESS by EXACT name; never `pkill -f` (self-SIGTERM hazard).
Resume by CONV_ID (newest jsonl), never the tmux window UUID.
Prewrite the bypass-version config (lastReleaseNotesSeen + projects[REPO].lastVersionBase = VER) after the kill; atomic temp+rename.
The helper is `setsid`-detached and `start-attach` runs in a native terminal (real TTY); a headless run is refused, not attempted.
Always surface the recovery command before launching.
execution-rules.note: HARDENING (recommended future work): promote this orchestration into a `tmux-cli self-upgrade` CLI command that reuses internal/setup + cmd/tmux-cli/self_update.go (dispatchSessionRestart already does buildâ†’stop-daemonâ†’kill-sessionâ†’self-exec start-attach; extend it to resolve CONV_ID + prewrite the version config + pass `--resume <id>`), so the flow is deterministic Go rather than an LLM-composed helper.
execution-rules.error-reporting: On a genuine harness/infra/spec defect (an MCP tool or `tmux-cli` command that errored after its own retries, a contradictory/under-specified goal spec, or a validator-error-class condition), follow the shared `error-reporting` procedure in `tmux/task-report.xml` â€” compose + submit autonomously, honoring api.enabled / DEDUP / RECURSION-guard. NEVER on expected work failures (red validate cycle, supervisor pushback, NEED_INPUT, unwritten-code test red) or daemon-owned goal-failures.
